cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must not have the "systemd-timesyncd" package installed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must not have the "ntp" package installed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must not have the telnet package installed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must not have the rsh-server package installed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must use a file integrity tool to verify correct operation of all security functions.

Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must notify designated personnel if baseline configurations are changed in an unauthorized manner. The file integrity tool must notify the system administrator (SA) when changes to the baseline configuration or anomalies in the operation of any security functions are discovered.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must be configured to preserve log records from failure events.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must have an application firewall installed in order to control remote access methods.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must have the "auditd" package installed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS audit event multiplexor must be configured to offload audit logs onto a different system or storage media from the system being audited.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must have AppArmor installed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must have the "libpam-pwquality" package installed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must have the "SSSD" package installed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must have the "chrony" package installed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must have SSH installed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must accept Personal Identity Verification (PIV) credentials.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must accept Personal Identity Verification (PIV) credentials managed through the Privileged Access Management (PAM)  framework.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must allow users to directly initiate a session lock for all connection types.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS when booted must require authentication upon booting into single-user and maintenance modes.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must initiate session audits at system startup.

files./boot/grub/grub.cfg:
before
linux   /vmlinuz-6.8.0-31-generic root=UUID=c92a542f-aee4-4af9-94b2-203624ccb8e3 ro audit=1 quiet splash $vt_handoff
after

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must limit the number of concurrent sessions to 10 for all accounts and/or account types.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must prevent a user from overriding the disabling of the graphical user interface automount function.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must automatically terminate a user session after inactivity timeouts have expired.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must disable account identifiers (individuals, groups, roles, and devices) after 35 days of inactivity.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must automatically lock an account until the locked account is released by an administrator when three unsuccessful logon attempts have been made.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must enable the graphical user logon banner to display the Standard Mandatory DOD Notice and Consent Banner before granting local access to the system via a graphical user logon.

check.pattern: banner-message-text="You are accessing a U.S. Government \(USG\) Information System \(IS\) that is provided for USG-authorized use only.\s+By using this IS \(which includes any device attached to this IS\), you consent to the following conditions:\s+-The USG routinely intercepts and monitors communications on this IS for purposes including, but not limited to, penetration testing, COMSEC monitoring, network operations and defense, personnel misconduct \(PM\), law enforcement \(LE\), and counterintelligence \(CI\) investigations.\s+-At any time, the USG may inspect and seize data stored on this IS.\s+-Communications using, or data stored on, this IS are not private, are subject to routine monitoring, interception, and search, and may be disclosed or used for any USG-authorized purpose.\s+-This IS includes security measures \(e.g., authentication and access controls\) to protect USG interests--not for your personal benefit or privacy.\s+-Notwithstanding the above, using this IS does not constitute consent to PM, LE or CI investigative searching or monitoring of the content of privileged communications, or work product, related to personal representation or services by attorneys, psychotherapists, or clergy, and their assistants. Such communications and work product are private and confidential. See User Agreement for details."

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must display the Standard Mandatory DOD Notice and Consent Banner before granting local access to the system via a graphical user logon.

files./etc/gdm3/greeter.dconf-defaults:
before
banner-message-text="You are accessing a U.S. Government \(USG\) Information System \(IS\) that is provided for USG-authorized use only.\s+By using this IS \(which includes any device attached to this IS\), you consent to the following conditions:\s+-The USG routinely intercepts and monitors communications on this IS for purposes including, but not limited to, penetration testing, COMSEC monitoring, network operations and defense, personnel misconduct \(PM\), law enforcement \(LE\), and counterintelligence \(CI\) investigations.\s+-At any time, the USG may inspect and seize data stored on this IS.\s+-Communications using, or data stored on, this IS are not private, are subject to routine monitoring, interception, and search, and may be disclosed or used for any USG-authorized purpose.\s+-This IS includes security measures \(e.g., authentication and access controls\) to protect USG interests--not for your personal benefit or privacy.\s+-Notwithstanding the above, using this IS does not constitute consent to PM, LE or CI investigative searching or monitoring of the content of privileged communications, or work product, related to personal representation or services by attorneys, psychotherapists, or clergy, and their assistants. Such communications and work product are private and confidential. See User Agreement for details."
after

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must prevent the use of dictionary words for passwords.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must be configured so that when passwords are changed or new passwords are established, pwquality must be used.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must enforce a delay of at least four seconds between logon prompts following a failed logon attempt.

files./etc/pam.d/common-auth:
before
auth    required    pam_faildelay.so    delay=4000000
after

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must display the date and time of the last successful account logon upon logon.

files./etc/pam.d/login:
before
session     required      pam_lastlog.so showfailed
after

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must not allow accounts configured in Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) with blank or null passwords.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS default filesystem permissions must be defined in such a way that all authenticated users can read and modify only their own files.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must disable automatic mounting of Universal Serial Bus (USB) mass storage driver.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must implement smart card logins for multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged and nonprivileged accounts.

check.pattern: | awk '/pkcs11_module opensc {/,/}/' /etc/pam_pkcs11/pam_pkcs11.conf | grep cert_policy | grep ocsp_on

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must electronically verify Personal Identity Verification (PIV) credentials.

files./etc/pam_pkcs11/pam_pkcs11.conf:
before
| awk '/pkcs11_module opensc {/,/}/' /etc/pam_pkcs11/pam_pkcs11.conf | grep cert_policy | grep ocsp_on
after

check.pattern: password [success=1 default=ignore] pam_unix.so obscure sha512 shadow rounds=100000

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must store only encrypted representations of passwords.

files./etc/pam.d/common-password:
before
password [success=1 default=ignore] pam_unix.so obscure sha512 shadow rounds=100000
after

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must enforce password complexity by requiring that at least one uppercase character be used.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must enforce password complexity by requiring that at least one lowercase character be used.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must enforce password complexity by requiring that at least one numeric character be used.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must require the change of at least eight characters when passwords are changed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must enforce 24 hours/1 day as the minimum password lifetime. Passwords for new users must have a 24 hours/1 day minimum password lifetime restriction.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must enforce a 60-day maximum password lifetime restriction. Passwords for new users must have a 60-day maximum password lifetime restriction.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must enforce a minimum 15-character password length.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must enforce password complexity by requiring that at least one special character be used.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must be configured such that Pluggable Authentication Module (PAM) prohibits the use of cached authentications after one day.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, for PKI-based authentication, SSSD must validate certificates by constructing a certification path (which includes status information) to an accepted trust anchor.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must map the authenticated identity to the user or group account for PKI-based authentication.

check.pattern: | awk '/pkcs11_module opensc {/,/}/' /etc/pam_pkcs11/pam_pkcs11.conf | grep cert_policy | grep ca

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, for PKI-based authentication, Privileged Access Management (PAM) must validate certificates by constructing a certification path (which includes status information) to an accepted trust anchor.

files./etc/pam_pkcs11/pam_pkcs11.conf:
before
| awk '/pkcs11_module opensc {/,/}/' /etc/pam_pkcs11/pam_pkcs11.conf | grep cert_policy | grep ca
after

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS for PKI-based authentication, must implement a local cache of revocation data in case of the inability to access revocation information via the network.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must encrypt all stored passwords with a FIPS 140-3 approved cryptographic hashing algorithm.

Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must implement NIST FIPS-validated cryptography to protect classified information and for the following: To provision digital signatures, to generate cryptographic hashes, and to protect unclassified information requiring confidentiality and cryptographic protection in accordance with applicable federal laws, Executive Orders, directives, policies, regulations, and standards.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must use DOD PKI-established certificate authorities (CAs) for verification of the establishment of protected sessions.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must ensure only users who need access to security functions are part of sudo group.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must restrict access to the kernel message buffer.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must synchronize internal information system clocks to the authoritative time source when the time difference is greater than one second.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must be configured to use TCP syncookies.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must configure the /var/log directory to have mode "0755" or less permissive.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must configure /var/log/syslog file with mode "0640" or less permissive.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must implement nonexecutable data to protect its memory from unauthorized code execution.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must implement address space layout randomization to protect its memory from unauthorized code execution.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must be configured so that Advance Package Tool (APT) removes all software components after updated versions have been installed.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must be a vendor-supported release.

check.pattern: -a always,exit -F path=/bin/su -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-priv_change

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must generate audit records for successful/unsuccessful uses of the su command.

files./bin/su:
before
-a always,exit -F path=/bin/su -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-priv_change
after

check.pattern: -a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/chfn -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-chfn

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must generate audit records for successful/unsuccessful uses of the chfn command.

files./usr/bin/chfn:
before
-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/chfn -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-chfn
after

check.pattern: -a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/mount -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-mount

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must generate audit records for successful/unsuccessful uses of the mount command.

files./usr/bin/mount:
before
-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/mount -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-mount
after

check.pattern: -a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/umount -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-umount

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must generate audit records for successful/unsuccessful uses of the umount command.

files./usr/bin/umount:
before
-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/umount -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-umount
after

check.pattern: -a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/ssh-agent -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-ssh

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must generate audit records for successful/unsuccessful uses of the ssh-agent command.

files./usr/bin/ssh-agent:
before
-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/ssh-agent -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-ssh
after

check.pattern: -a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/sudo -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k priv_cmd

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must generate audit records for successful/unsuccessful uses of the sudo command.

files./usr/bin/sudo:
before
-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/sudo -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k priv_cmd
after

check.pattern: -a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/sudoedit -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k priv_cmd

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must generate audit records for successful/unsuccessful uses of the sudoedit command.

files./usr/bin/sudoedit:
before
-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/sudoedit -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k priv_cmd
after

cases.description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must allocate audit record storage capacity to store at least one week's worth of audit records, when audit records are not immediately sent to a central audit record storage facility.

Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must immediately notify the system administrator (SA) and information system security officer (ISSO) (at a minimum) when allocated audit record storage volume reaches 75 percent of the repository maximum audit record storage capacity.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must alert the system administrator (SA) and information system security officer (ISSO) (at a minimum) in the event of an audit processing failure.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must configure audit tools with a mode of "0755" or less permissive.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must be configured so that audit log files are not read or write-accessible by unauthorized users.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must be configured to permit only authorized users ownership of the audit log files.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must permit only authorized groups ownership of the audit log files.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must be configured so that the audit log directory is not write-accessible by unauthorized users.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS audit system must protect auditing rules from unauthorized change.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must restrict privilege elevation to authorized personnel.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must audit any script or executable called by cron as root or by any privileged user.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must be a vendor-supported release.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must not have the nfs-kernel-server package installed.
